rows.itemTitle: OBLIGATION TO PARTICIPATE IN THE ACTUAL OPERATION OF THE FRANCHISE BUSINESS
rows.summary: The obligation to personally operate the franchise shifted from a general 'best full-time efforts' and 'active involvement' standard (2016) to a rigid, time-bound, role-specific staffing mandate requiring five named positions and mandating the franchisee’s physical removal from operational roles after six months (2017). Non-compete scope expanded to include spouse and all equity holders, and confidentiality obligations were newly extended to all employees — not just managerial or ownership-tier personnel.

(1) all supervisorial or managerial personnel of yours, (2) all officers, directors, members, partners, general partners, limited partners and holders of a beneficial interest of yours, and (3) the spouse of each such person

You must at all times, starting from the opening of your center, employ a (1) Center Director, (2) Program Director, (3) Assistant Director, (4) Sensory Motor Child Achievement Coach, and (5) Cognitive Academic Teacher

rows.itemTitle: OBLIGATION TO PARTICIPATE IN THE ACTUAL OPERATION OF THE FRANCHISE BUSINESS
rows.summary: The personal guaranty obligation was significantly expanded: (1) all owners—regardless of operational involvement—must now sign a personal guaranty; (2) the guaranty explicitly covers 'all obligations under the Franchise Agreement' (not just 'obligations of the Franchised Business'); (3) guaranty requirements now extend to Area Development Agreements with parallel, reinforced language; (4) clarified spousal guaranty is triggered only by ownership interest, not marital status alone.

oldEvidence.quote: If you are a business entity, your Owners must sign the Owner’s Guaranty attached to the Franchise Agreement.
If your spouse is an Owner of the Franchised Business, your spouse must sign a personal guaranty making such spouse individually liable for all obligations of the Franchised Business

newEvidence.quote: If you are a business entity, all your Owners (whether or not they are involved in the operation of the Franchised Business) must sign a personal guaranty, which is the Owner’s Guaranty attached to the Franchise Agreement, making each Owner individually liable for all obligations under the Franchise Agreement.

If your spouse is an Owner of the business entity, your spouse must sign an Owner’s Guaranty making such spouse individually liable for all obligations under the Franchise Agreement whether or not such spouse is involved in the operation of the business entity. A spouse of an Owner is not required to sign an Owner’s Guaranty if the spouse has no ownership interest in the business entity.

If we enter into an Area Development Agreement with you, you must designate one individual as the responsible owner... your Owners (whether or not they are involved in the development of the Development Area) must sign a personal guaranty, which is the Owner’s Guaranty attached to the Area Development Agreement.
